entries.title: About Students take folding to another level as they learn key techniques such as pleated surfaces, curved folding and crumpling. These techniques give them the tools to express their creativity and design their own models.
entries.label: Students take folding to another level as they learn key techniques such as pleated surfaces, curved folding and crumpling. These techniques give them the tools to express their creativity and design their own models.
entries.content:
Sloyd: Level 7
Level 7 starts back at the beginning of sloyd with folding techniques. However

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: these folding techniques are of a higher thinking level

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: encouraging students to envision these techniques into building structures.  Paul Jackson has created videos to go along with some of the techniques

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: but students are encouraged to read the instructions carefully and figure these things out based on what they have learned in the previous models. While sloyd is beneficial for all students to envision geometrical forms in reality

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Level 7 will be particularly interesting to the students who wish to go on to higher-level maths

entries.title: Students take folding to another level as they learn key techniques such as pleated surfaces, curved folding and crumpling. These techniques give them the tools to express their creativity and design their own models.
entries.label: Students take folding to another level as they learn key techniques such as pleated surfaces, curved folding and crumpling. These techniques give them the tools to express their creativity and design their own models.
entries.content:
Grade 8 students who are new to sloyd should begin with Sloyd: Level 1 and then continue courses in sequence.

Prerequisite: Sloyd: Level 6
